purpose: Add/record diagnostics for feat eligibility, choice resolution, pending entitlements, and identify feats that need a player-facing choice upon selection.
notes: This report does not rewrite feat mechanics. It identifies choice-required and choice-dependent feats for Phase 6 implementation/backfill.
A choice upon selection is different from a choice made later when the feat is activated, targets an enemy, or modifies a roll.
Grouped Weapon Proficiency requires a prompt; fixed variants such as Weapon Proficiency (Pistols) do not because the chosen group is already encoded in the feat row.
No additional standalone scraped feat-description input was visible in /mnt/data at generation time; existing repo scrape reports under tools/ were cross-referenced.

highPriorityNextBackfills.name: Double Attack
highPriorityNextBackfills.shortSummary: Make an extra attack with chosen weapon during Full Attack, -5 penalty to all attacks.
highPriorityNextBackfills.benefitPreview: Choose a single Exotic Weapon or weapon group with which you are proficient. When you use the Full Attack action, you may make one additional attack when wielding such a weapon. However, you take a -5 penalty on all attack rolls until your next turn because you're trading precisi
why: Choose one proficient weapon group or exotic weapon.

highPriorityNextBackfills.name: Recurring Success
highPriorityNextBackfills.shortSummary: Gain extra use of a 1/encounter Talent or Feat.
highPriorityNextBackfills.benefitPreview: When you select this Feat, choose one Talent or Feat that can normally be used once per encounter. You can use that Talent or Feat one additional time per encounter.
why: When selected, choose one once-per-encounter talent or feat.

highPriorityNextBackfills.name: Educated
highPriorityNextBackfills.benefitPreview: Choose two Knowledge skills. You gain a +5 bonus on checks with those skills.
why: Choose two Knowledge skills; Phase 3 disabled hard-coded bonuses until choices are stored.

highPriorityNextBackfills.name: Triple Attack
highPriorityNextBackfills.prerequisite: Base Attack Bonus +11, Double Attack (Chosen Weapon)
highPriorityNextBackfills.shortSummary: Make second extra attack with chosen weapon during Full Attack, additional -5 penalty to all attacks.
highPriorityNextBackfills.benefitPreview: Choose a single Exotic Weapon or weapon group. When you use the Full Attack Action, you may make one additional attack when wielding such a Weapon. However, you take a -5 penalty on all attack rolls until your next turn because you're trading precision for speed. The extra attack
why: Choose one weapon group or exotic weapon; should usually derive/validate from Double Attack.

highPriorityNextBackfills.name: Superior Tech
highPriorityNextBackfills.benefitPreview: Select one of the following: Armor, Weapons, Droids, Vehicles, or Devices. You can install advanced Traits when modifying the selected type of equipment. These Traits replace the Traits granted by the Tech Specialist Feat. You may still use the Tech Specialist options if you so c
why: Select one category: Armor, Weapons, Droids, Vehicles, or Devices.

highPriorityNextBackfills.name: Inborn Resilience
highPriorityNextBackfills.shortSummary: Reduce one Defense bonus but increase another.
highPriorityNextBackfills.benefitPreview: Reduce one Defense bonus but increase another.
why: User identified this as a selection feat; rules require choosing one species Defense bonus to reduce and another to increase.

mappedImmediateChoices.name: Skill Focus
mappedImmediateChoices.shortSummary: Gain +5 competence bonus on Skill Checks with one Trained Skill.
mappedImmediateChoices.benefitPreview: Choose one Skill in which you are Trained. You gain a +5 competence bonus on all Skill Checks made with that Skill.
why: choiceMeta.required true
choiceMeta.resolution immediate

mappedImmediateChoices.name: Exotic Weapon Proficiency

choiceMeta.notes: Legacy feat row retained for compatibility; future UI should prefer the grouped Weapon Proficiency feat exotic branch.

mappedImmediateChoices.shortSummary: Wield an Exotic Weapon without penalty.
mappedImmediateChoices.benefitPreview: Select a single weapon from either the Exotic Weapons (Melee) or Exotic Weapons (Ranged) weapon groups; you make attack rolls with the selected weapon without penalty.
why: choiceMeta.required true
choiceMeta.resolution immediate

mappedImmediateChoices.name: Greater Weapon Specialization
mappedImmediateChoices.prerequisite: Weapon Specialization with selected weapon group, base attack bonus +12
mappedImmediateChoices.shortSummary: You gain a +2 bonus on damage rolls with the selected weapon group.
mappedImmediateChoices.benefitPreview: You gain a +2 bonus on damage rolls with the selected weapon group. This stacks with Weapon Specialization.
why: choiceMeta.required true
choiceMeta.resolution immediate

mappedImmediateChoices.prerequisite: Trained in Mechanics and Use Computer
mappedImmediateChoices.shortSummary: +1 bonus to certain Skills and Defenses when dealing with a selected class of Droid.

You gain a +1 bonus to Deception, Mechanics, Perception, Persuasion, and Use Computer checks when used on or against a Droid of the selected degree, and a +1 bonus to all Defenses against at

mappedDeferredGrantChoices.name: Starship Tactics
mappedDeferredGrantChoices.shortSummary: You are trained to make use of Starship Maneuvers and are skilled at space combat.
mappedDeferredGrantChoices.benefitPreview: You add to your Starship Maneuver Suite a number of Starship Maneuvers equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). You can add the same Starship Maneuver more than once.
why: choiceMeta.required true
choiceMeta.resolution grant_entitlement

mappedDeferredGrantChoices.name: Linguist
mappedDeferredGrantChoices.shortSummary: Gain bonus Languages equal to 1 + your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).
mappedDeferredGrantChoices.benefitPreview: You gain a number of additional Languages equal to 1 + your Intelligence modifier (Minimum 1).
why: choiceMeta.required true
choiceMeta.resolution grant_entitlement

mappedDeferredGrantChoices.name: Force Training
mappedDeferredGrantChoices.prerequisite: Force Sensitivity, Trained in Use the Force.
mappedDeferredGrantChoices.shortSummary: Learn a number of Force Powers equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).
mappedDeferredGrantChoices.benefitPreview: You add to your Force Power Suite a number of Force Powers equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier (Minimum 1). You can add the same Force Power more than once.
why: choiceMeta.required true
choiceMeta.resolution grant_entitlement

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Power Attack
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: Trade attack bonus for damage on melee attacks (up to your Base Attack Bonus).
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: On your turn, before making an attack roll, you may choose to subtract a number from all melee attack rolls and add the same number to all melee damage rolls. This number may not exceed your Base Attack Bonus. The penalty on attacks and bonus on damage rolls applies until the sta
why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Targeted Area
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: Deal an additional 5 points of damage against one target within a successful Area Attack.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: When you make a successful Area Attack with a weapon with which you are proficient, select a target hit by the attack. That target takes an additional 5 points of damage from the attack, before applying the effect of the Evasion Talent.

why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Forceful Recovery
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: Regain one Force Power when you catch a Second Wind.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: Whenever you catch a Second Wind, choose one expended Force Power and return that power to your Force Power Suite.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Stay Up
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: Move 1 step down Condition Track to reduce damage.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: Once per encounter, when you would normally take damage from an attack, you can instead choose to take half damage and move -1 step along the Condition Track.
why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Fatal Hit
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: You automatically kill an enemy that you reduce to 0 Hit Points, and you can perform a Coup de Grace as a Standard Action.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: When your attack drops a target to 0 Hit Points, you may choose to automatically kill that enemy even if the damage does not exceed the target's Damage Threshold. In addition, you can perform the Coup de Grace Action as a Standard Action.
why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Impetuous Move
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: Move when you catch a Second Wind.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: When you catch a Second Wind, you can choose to regain only half of the Hit Points you normally would with a Second Wind and immediately Move up to half of your Speed. This movement does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity.
why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Brink of Death
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: Attacks that would kill your enemy reduces them to 0 Hit Points instead.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: When you deal sufficient damage on an attack to kill a target, you may instead choose to reduce the target to 0 Hit Points, leaving it Unconscious but alive. Normal rules for being at 0 Hit Points apply.
why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.name: Friendly Fire Avoidance
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: You may choose to exclude a number of squares equal to your Dexterity modifier from the area of effect of your autofire attacks.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: You may choose to exclude a number of squares equal to your Dexterity modifier from the area of effect of your autofire attacks.
why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.shortSummary: Push opponent 1 square after making a successful melee attack.
runtimeChoicesNotFeatSelectionExamples.benefitPreview: After making a successful melee attack against an opponent up to one size category larger than you, you can choose to move that opponent 1 square in any direction as a Free Action. You can't Bantha Rush an opponent that's being Grabbed or Grappled, and you can't Bantha Rush your
why: Choice/selection language occurs during activation, targeting, or encounter use; do not prompt during feat acquisition.

scrapeStatus.note: I did not see the additional scraped files in /mnt/data during this Phase 5 generation. If they are supplied in a later snapshot, rerun this audit and merge those descriptions into the Phase 1 gap list.
